REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MIU LEE, VALERIE LEE, DARREN LEE, AND EMPRESS EXPRESS, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO REFUND SECURITY DEPOSIT PER CC 1950.5(G)(1), NEGLIGENT AND INTENTIONAL VIOLATION OF STATUTORY DUTY, VIOLATION OF SFRO 37.10B - THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BARRED BY THE APPLICABLE STATUTES OF LIMITATIONS PER CCP 338 AND CCP 340. MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH FACTS IN SUPPORT OF EVERY ELEMENT OF DELAYED DISCOVERY. MOTION IS OTHERWISE DENIED AS THE ARGUMENTS AS TO THE REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION SUBJECT TO THIS MOTION ARE IN PART BASED ON UNPLED AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE.PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: ANDREW Y. S. CHENG, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Off Calendar Mar-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MIU LEE, VALERIE LEE, DARREN LEE, and EMPRESS EXPRESS, INC.'s Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer To First Amended Complaint

OFF CALENDAR. Motion has not been served on all parties to this action, specifically Hung Lee; counsel of record Utrecht & Lenvin, LLP. See Proof of Service filed on February 2, 2017.
Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Mar-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MIU LEE, VALERIE LEE, DARREN LEE, and EMPRESS EXPRESS, INC.'s MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted without leave to amend as to causes of action for Failure to Refund Security Deposit per CC 1950.5(g)(1), Negligent and Intentional Violation of Statutory Duty, Violation of SFRO 37.10B - these causes of action are barred by the applicable statutes of limitations per CCP 338 and CCP 340. Motion is granted with leave to amend as to fraud cause of action for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts in support of every element of delayed discovery. Motion is otherwise denied as the arguments as to the remaining causes of action subject to this motion are in part based on unpled affirmative defense. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)
